toolUseResult.content:
# voice-memos

A [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) skill that turns Apple Voice Memos into transcripts and summaries — entirely on-device, using your Mac's Apple Silicon GPU.

> **Apple Silicon Macs only (M1 / M2 / M3 / M4).** This skill uses PyTorch's MPS backend to run the transcription model on the GPU built into your chip. It will not work on Intel Macs or any non-Apple platform.

---

## What it does

| Command | What happens |
|---|---|
| `/voice-memos` | Lists all your Voice Memos (title, date, duration) |
| `/voice-memos <title>` | Transcribes + summarizes that memo |
| `/voice-memos <title> --qa` | Same, with a dedicated Q&A highlights section |
| `/voice-memos <title> --speakers "Alice (moderator), Bob (guest)"` | Speaker-attributed transcript and per-person summary |
| `/voice-memos search <keyword>` | Searches across memos for a topic |

Follow-up questions work naturally in the same conversation — the transcript is cached so nothing gets re-transcribed.

---

## How it works

```
/voice-memos "my last recording"
        │
        ▼
  SKILL.md (orchestrator)
  ├─ queries CloudRecordings.db via sqlite3
  ├─ fuzzy-matches your query to a memo title
  └─ routes to the right sub-agent
              │
              ▼
        agents/transcribe-memo.md
        ├─ resolves full .m4a path
        ├─ runs transcribe_cohere_mps.py via env vars
        │     └─ ffmpeg  →  raw PCM  →  20s chunks
        │           └─  CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026 (MPS, fp16)
        │                 ~46× real-time speed
        ├─ caches full transcript in conversation context
        └─ Claude summarizes + attributes speakers
```

The transcription script runs locally — your audio never leaves your machine.

---

## Requirements

### Hardware
- **Apple Silicon Mac** — M1, M2, M3, or M4 (any variant: Pro, Max, Ultra)
- macOS 13 Ventura or later

### System tools
| Tool | Install | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| **ffmpeg** | `brew install ffmpeg` | Decodes `.m4a` audio to raw PCM |
| **sqlite3** | Pre-installed on macOS | Queries the Voice Memos database |
| **Python 3.11+** | `brew install python@3.14` (recommended) | Runs the transcription script |
| **Homebrew** | [brew.sh](https://brew.sh) | Package manager for ffmpeg + Python |

### Python packages
All listed in `requirements.txt`. Key dependencies:

| Package | Version | Why it's needed |
|---------|---------|-----------------|
| `torch` | ≥ 2.11.0 | PyTorch — MPS (Metal) backend for GPU inference |
| `transformers` | ≥ 5.4.0 | Loads and runs `CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026` |
| `accelerate` | ≥ 1.13.0 | Mixed-precision dispatch; required by transformers for MPS |
| `safetensors` | ≥ 0.7.0 | Fast, safe model weight loading |
| `tokenizers` | ≥ 0.22.2 | Fast tokenizer (transformers dependency) |
| `sentencepiece` | ≥ 0.2.1 | SentencePiece tokenizer support |
| `numpy` | ≥ 2.0.0 | PCM audio array operations |
| `librosa` | ≥ 0.11.0 | Audio loading and resampling |
| `soundfile` | ≥ 0.13.0 | WAV/FLAC/AIFF read-write |
| `scipy` | ≥ 1.17.0 | Signal processing (librosa dependency) |
| `huggingface_hub[cli]` | ≥ 1.8.0 | `huggingface-cli` — downloads the model from HF Hub |
| `hf-xet` | ≥ 1.4.0 | XetHub transfer acceleration for large weight files |
| `tqdm` | ≥ 4.67.0 | Download progress bars |

### Transcription model
- **Model**: `CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026` (~3 GB)
- **Hosted on**: [Hugging Face Hub](https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026)
- **Requires**: A free HuggingFace account + access token to download
- **Cached at**: `~/.cache/huggingface/` after first download — subsequent runs load from disk

### macOS permission
- **Full Disk Access** for Terminal (or your terminal app): required to read the Voice Memos database and `.m4a` files stored in `~/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/`.
  - Go to: **System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → enable your terminal**

### Claude Code
- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) installed and working
- The skill files must be placed in `~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/` (done automatically by `setup.sh`)

---

## Installation

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/voice-memos.git
cd voice-memos
```

### 2. Run setup

```bash
bash setup.sh
```

This will:
- Confirm you're on Apple Silicon
- Install `ffmpeg` via Homebrew (if missing)
- Create a Python virtual environment at `~/cohere_env`
- Install all Python dependencies from `requirements.txt`
- Copy the skill files to `~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/`
- Guide you through HuggingFace login

### 3. Log in to HuggingFace

```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login
```

Paste a token from [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens). A free account is sufficient.

**Pre-download the model** (recommended — avoids a 3 GB download on first `/voice-memos` call):

```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
```

### 4. Grant Full Disk Access

**System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → enable Terminal** (or iTerm2, Ghostty, etc.)

### 5. Use it

```
/voice-memos
```

---

## Manual installation (no setup.sh)

If you prefer to set up manually:

```bash
# 1. Install system deps
brew install ffmpeg python@3.14

# 2. Create venv
python3.14 -m venv ~/cohere_env
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

# 3. Log in to HuggingFace
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login

# 4. Pre-download model (optional but recommended)
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026

# 5. Install skill
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents
cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
chmod +x ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

If your venv is **not** at `~/cohere_env`, update the Python path in:
- `agents/transcribe-memo.md` → Step 3 transcription command
- `agents/search-memos.md` → Step 3 transcription command

---

## Usage examples

### List all memos
```
/voice-memos
/voice-memos list
/voice-memos what recordings do I have
```

### Summarize a specific memo
```
/voice-memos project kickoff
/voice-memos my most recent recording
/voice-memos Jan 15 standup
```

### Speaker attribution
```
/voice-memos podcast interview --speakers "Alice (host), Bob (guest)"
/voice-memos alice and bob interview
```

### With Q&A section
```
/voice-memos conference talk --qa
/voice-memos all-hands meeting --qa
```

### Combined
```
/voice-memos product demo --speakers "Alice (PM), Bob (engineer)" --qa
```

### Search across memos
```
/voice-memos search deployment strategy
/voice-memos find recordings about the Q4 budget
```

---

## Performance

Tested on Apple Silicon using the MPS (Metal Performance Shaders) backend at fp16 precision:

| Recording length | Approx. transcription time |
|---|---|
| 5 minutes | ~7 seconds |
| 15 minutes | ~20 seconds |
| 30 minutes | ~40 seconds |
| 60 minutes | ~80 seconds |
| 78 minutes | ~2 minutes |

~46× real-time speed. Model loads in ~15 seconds on first use per session; subsequent transcriptions in the same session skip the load step.

---

## Repo structure

```
voice-memos/
├── SKILL.md                    # Claude Code skill entry point (orchestrator)
├── transcribe_cohere_mps.py    # MPS transcription script
├── requirements.txt            # Python dependencies
├── setup.sh                    # One-command setup
├── .gitignore
└── agents/
    ├── list-memos.md           # Sub-agent: list all memos
    ├── transcribe-memo.md      # Sub-agent: transcribe + summarize one memo
    └── search-memos.md         # Sub-agent: keyword search across memos
```

---

## Troubleshooting

**`MPS not available`**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"
# Must print: True
```
If False, verify you're on Apple Silicon and using a native (arm64) Python, not Rosetta.

**`sqlite3: unable to open database`**
Terminal doesn't have Full Disk Access. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access.

**`No such file or directory` for audio**
The env-var pattern (`AUDIO_PATH="..." python ...`) handles spaces in filenames safely. Make sure the path is correct with `ls "$HOME/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/"`.

**HuggingFace auth error**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login
```

**`Input type (float) and bias type (c10::Half)`**
Update transformers: `~/cohere_env/bin/pip install "transformers>=5.4.0"`.

**Voice Memos DB is locked (busy)**
```bash
cp "$HOME/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/CloudRecordings.db" /tmp/vm_query.db
sqlite3 /tmp/vm_query.db "SELECT ..."
```

---

## Privacy

All processing is local. Your audio files, transcripts, and Voice Memos database never leave your machine. The only network call is the one-time model download from HuggingFace.

---

## License

MIT

file.content:
# voice-memos

A [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) skill that turns Apple Voice Memos into transcripts and summaries — entirely on-device, using your Mac's Apple Silicon GPU.

> **Apple Silicon Macs only (M1 / M2 / M3 / M4).** This skill uses PyTorch's MPS backend to run the transcription model on the GPU built into your chip. It will not work on Intel Macs or any non-Apple platform.

---

## What it does

| Command | What happens |
|---|---|
| `/voice-memos` | Lists all your Voice Memos (title, date, duration) |
| `/voice-memos <title>` | Transcribes + summarizes that memo |
| `/voice-memos <title> --qa` | Same, with a dedicated Q&A highlights section |
| `/voice-memos <title> --speakers "Alice (moderator), Bob (guest)"` | Speaker-attributed transcript and per-person summary |
| `/voice-memos search <keyword>` | Searches across memos for a topic |

Follow-up questions work naturally in the same conversation — the transcript is cached so nothing gets re-transcribed.

---

## How it works

```
/voice-memos "my last recording"
        │
        ▼
  SKILL.md (orchestrator)
  ├─ queries CloudRecordings.db via sqlite3
  ├─ fuzzy-matches your query to a memo title
  └─ routes to the right sub-agent
              │
              ▼
        agents/transcribe-memo.md
        ├─ resolves full .m4a path
        ├─ runs transcribe_cohere_mps.py via env vars
        │     └─ ffmpeg  →  raw PCM  →  20s chunks
        │           └─  CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026 (MPS, fp16)
        │                 ~46× real-time speed
        ├─ caches full transcript in conversation context
        └─ Claude summarizes + attributes speakers
```

The transcription script runs locally — your audio never leaves your machine.

---

## Requirements

### Hardware
- **Apple Silicon Mac** — M1, M2, M3, or M4 (any variant: Pro, Max, Ultra)
- macOS 13 Ventura or later

### System tools
| Tool | Install | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| **ffmpeg** | `brew install ffmpeg` | Decodes `.m4a` audio to raw PCM |
| **sqlite3** | Pre-installed on macOS | Queries the Voice Memos database |
| **Python 3.11+** | `brew install python@3.14` (recommended) | Runs the transcription script |
| **Homebrew** | [brew.sh](https://brew.sh) | Package manager for ffmpeg + Python |

### Python packages
All listed in `requirements.txt`. Key dependencies:

| Package | Version | Why it's needed |
|---------|---------|-----------------|
| `torch` | ≥ 2.11.0 | PyTorch — MPS (Metal) backend for GPU inference |
| `transformers` | ≥ 5.4.0 | Loads and runs `CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026` |
| `accelerate` | ≥ 1.13.0 | Mixed-precision dispatch; required by transformers for MPS |
| `safetensors` | ≥ 0.7.0 | Fast, safe model weight loading |
| `tokenizers` | ≥ 0.22.2 | Fast tokenizer (transformers dependency) |
| `sentencepiece` | ≥ 0.2.1 | SentencePiece tokenizer support |
| `numpy` | ≥ 2.0.0 | PCM audio array operations |
| `librosa` | ≥ 0.11.0 | Audio loading and resampling |
| `soundfile` | ≥ 0.13.0 | WAV/FLAC/AIFF read-write |
| `scipy` | ≥ 1.17.0 | Signal processing (librosa dependency) |
| `huggingface_hub[cli]` | ≥ 1.8.0 | `huggingface-cli` — downloads the model from HF Hub |
| `hf-xet` | ≥ 1.4.0 | XetHub transfer acceleration for large weight files |
| `tqdm` | ≥ 4.67.0 | Download progress bars |

### Transcription model
- **Model**: `CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026` (~3 GB)
- **Hosted on**: [Hugging Face Hub](https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026)
- **Requires**: A free HuggingFace account + access token to download
- **Cached at**: `~/.cache/huggingface/` after first download — subsequent runs load from disk

### macOS permission
- **Full Disk Access** for Terminal (or your terminal app): required to read the Voice Memos database and `.m4a` files stored in `~/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/`.
  - Go to: **System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → enable your terminal**

### Claude Code
- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) installed and working
- The skill files must be placed in `~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/` (done automatically by `setup.sh`)

---

## Installation

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/voice-memos.git
cd voice-memos
```

### 2. Run setup

```bash
bash setup.sh
```

This will:
- Confirm you're on Apple Silicon
- Install `ffmpeg` via Homebrew (if missing)
- Create a Python virtual environment at `~/cohere_env`
- Install all Python dependencies from `requirements.txt`
- Copy the skill files to `~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/`
- Guide you through HuggingFace login

### 3. Log in to HuggingFace

```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login
```

Paste a token from [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens). A free account is sufficient.

**Pre-download the model** (recommended — avoids a 3 GB download on first `/voice-memos` call):

```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
```

### 4. Grant Full Disk Access

**System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → enable Terminal** (or iTerm2, Ghostty, etc.)

### 5. Use it

```
/voice-memos
```

---

## Manual installation (no setup.sh)

If you prefer to set up manually:

```bash
# 1. Install system deps
brew install ffmpeg python@3.14

# 2. Create venv
python3.14 -m venv ~/cohere_env
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

# 3. Log in to HuggingFace
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login

# 4. Pre-download model (optional but recommended)
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026

# 5. Install skill
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents
cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
chmod +x ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

If your venv is **not** at `~/cohere_env`, update the Python path in:
- `agents/transcribe-memo.md` → Step 3 transcription command
- `agents/search-memos.md` → Step 3 transcription command

---

## Usage examples

### List all memos
```
/voice-memos
/voice-memos list
/voice-memos what recordings do I have
```

### Summarize a specific memo
```
/voice-memos project kickoff
/voice-memos my most recent recording
/voice-memos Jan 15 standup
```

### Speaker attribution
```
/voice-memos podcast interview --speakers "Alice (host), Bob (guest)"
/voice-memos alice and bob interview
```

### With Q&A section
```
/voice-memos conference talk --qa
/voice-memos all-hands meeting --qa
```

### Combined
```
/voice-memos product demo --speakers "Alice (PM), Bob (engineer)" --qa
```

### Search across memos
```
/voice-memos search deployment strategy
/voice-memos find recordings about the Q4 budget
```

---

## Performance

Tested on Apple Silicon using the MPS (Metal Performance Shaders) backend at fp16 precision:

| Recording length | Approx. transcription time |
|---|---|
| 5 minutes | ~7 seconds |
| 15 minutes | ~20 seconds |
| 30 minutes | ~40 seconds |
| 60 minutes | ~80 seconds |
| 78 minutes | ~2 minutes |

~46× real-time speed. Model loads in ~15 seconds on first use per session; subsequent transcriptions in the same session skip the load step.

---

## Repo structure

```
voice-memos/
├── SKILL.md                    # Claude Code skill entry point (orchestrator)
├── transcribe_cohere_mps.py    # MPS transcription script
├── requirements.txt            # Python dependencies
├── setup.sh                    # One-command setup
├── .gitignore
└── agents/
    ├── list-memos.md           # Sub-agent: list all memos
    ├── transcribe-memo.md      # Sub-agent: transcribe + summarize one memo
    └── search-memos.md         # Sub-agent: keyword search across memos
```

---

## Troubleshooting

**`MPS not available`**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"
# Must print: True
```
If False, verify you're on Apple Silicon and using a native (arm64) Python, not Rosetta.

**`sqlite3: unable to open database`**
Terminal doesn't have Full Disk Access. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access.

**`No such file or directory` for audio**
The env-var pattern (`AUDIO_PATH="..." python ...`) handles spaces in filenames safely. Make sure the path is correct with `ls "$HOME/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/"`.

**HuggingFace auth error**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login
```

**`Input type (float) and bias type (c10::Half)`**
Update transformers: `~/cohere_env/bin/pip install "transformers>=5.4.0"`.

**Voice Memos DB is locked (busy)**
```bash
cp "$HOME/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/CloudRecordings.db" /tmp/vm_query.db
sqlite3 /tmp/vm_query.db "SELECT ..."
```

---

## Privacy

All processing is local. Your audio files, transcripts, and Voice Memos database never leave your machine. The only network call is the one-time model download from HuggingFace.

---

## License

MIT

[USER] # Update Config Skill

Modify Claude Code configuration by updating settings.json files.

## When Hooks Are Required (Not Memory)

If the user wants something to happen automatically in response to an EVENT, they need a **hook** configured in settings.json. Memory/preferences cannot trigger automated actions.

**These require hooks:**
- "Before compacting, ask me what to preserve" → PreCompact hook
- "After writing files, run prettier" → PostToolUse hook with Write|Edit matcher
- "When I run bash commands, log them" → PreToolUse hook with Bash matcher
- "Always run tests after code changes" → PostToolUse hook

**Hook events:** PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, Stop, Notification, SessionStart

## CRITICAL: Read Before Write

**Always read the existing settings file before making changes.** Merge new settings with existing ones - never replace the entire file.

## CRITICAL: Use AskUserQuestion for Ambiguity

When the user's request is ambiguous, use AskUserQuestion to clarify:
- Which settings file to modify (user/project/local)
- Whether to add to existing arrays or replace them
- Specific values when multiple options exist

## Decision: Config Tool vs Direct Edit

**Use the Config tool** for these simple settings:
- `theme`, `editorMode`, `verbose`, `model`
- `language`, `alwaysThinkingEnabled`
- `permissions.defaultMode`

**Edit settings.json directly** for:
- Hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.)
- Complex permission rules (allow/deny arrays)
- Environment variables
- MCP server configuration
- Plugin configuration

## Workflow

1. **Clarify intent** - Ask if the request is ambiguous
2. **Read existing file** - Use Read tool on the target settings file
3. **Merge carefully** - Preserve existing settings, especially arrays
4. **Edit file** - Use Edit tool (if file doesn't exist, ask user to create it first)
5. **Confirm** - Tell user what was changed

## Merging Arrays (Important!)

When adding to permission arrays or hook arrays, **merge with exis

### **📊 Project Overview**

[USER] # Update Config Skill

Modify Claude Code configuration by updating settings.json files.

## When Hooks Are Required (Not Memory)

If the user wants something to happen automatically in response to an EVENT, they need a **hook** configured in settings.json. Memory/preferences cannot trigger automated actions.

**These require hooks:**
- "Before compacting, ask me what to preserve" → PreCompact hook
- "After writing files, run prettier" → PostToolUse hook with Write|Edit matcher
- "When I run bash commands, log them" → PreToolUse hook with Bash matcher
- "Always run tests after code changes" → PostToolUse hook

**Hook events:** PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, Stop, Notification, SessionStart

## CRITICAL: Read Before Write

**Always read the existing settings file before making changes.** Merge new settings with existing ones - never replace the entire file.

## CRITICAL: Use AskUserQuestion for Ambiguity

When the user's request is ambiguous, use AskUserQuestion to clarify:
- Which settings file to modify (user/project/local)
- Whether to add to existing arrays or replace them
- Specific values when multiple options exist

## Decision: Config Tool vs Direct Edit

**Use the Config tool** for these simple settings:
- `theme`, `editorMode`, `verbose`, `model`
- `language`, `alwaysThinkingEnabled`
- `permissions.defaultMode`

**Edit settings.json directly** for:
- Hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.)
- Complex permission rules (allow/deny arrays)
- Environment variables
- MCP server configuration
- Plugin configuration

## Workflow

1. **Clarify intent** - Ask if the request is ambiguous
2. **Read existing file** - Use Read tool on the target settings file
3. **Merge carefully** - Preserve existing settings, especially arrays
4. **Edit file** - Use Edit tool (if file doesn't exist, ask user to create it first)
5. **Confirm** - Tell user what was changed

When adding to permission arrays or hook arrays, **merge with exis

**Key problems Claude on mac-studio made:**

1. **Wrong PyTorch version installed**: Claude installed PyTorch 2.2.2, but the skill requires `torch>=2.11.0`. These are very different — 2.2.2 is an older version from early 2024, while 2.11.0 is the actual requirement (released March 2026). Claude assumed 2.11.0 didn't exist without checking, then installed the wrong version.


2. **Wrong Python version**: Claude used Python 3.9 instead of the required 3.14 or at least 3.11+, which is significantly outdated.
3. **Wrong venv path**: The venv should be at `~/cohere_env/bin/python`, but Claude created it in a different location entirely.
4. **Rosetta confusion**: Claude misunderstood the machine's architecture — the M2 Ultra should run natively in ARM64, not under Rosetta emulation.
5. **PyTorch version mismatch**: MPS works with PyTorch 2.2.2, but the transformers library (5.4.0) needs torch 2.11.0+, and the Cohere model likely requires APIs only available in that newer version.
6. **NumPy compatibility**: There's a version mismatch warning suggesting numpy 2.0.0+ wasn't properly installed.
7. **Model not downloaded**: The dependencies were installed but the HuggingFace model itself was never actually downloaded or authenticated.
8. **Virtual environment path matters**: The skill files hardcode `~/cohere_env/bin/python`, so the venv has to be at exactly that location or the skill won't work.
9. **Transformers version issue**: The skill requires transformers 5.4.0 (a very recent March 2026 version), and an older version may have been installed instead.
10. **Model requirements**: The `cohere-transcribe-03-2026` model needs both torch 2.11.0+ and transformers 5.4.0+ to load properly.

content.thinking:
Now I have the complete picture of what went wrong:

1. **Python 3.9.6** — from Xcode's bundled Python, not the correct version (needs 3.11+)
2. **`cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg` points to Xcode Python** - `/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Library/Frameworks/Python3.framework/Versions/3.9/bin`
3. **PyTorch was installed as x86_64** — the dylib is `x86_64` but machine is arm64. This means PyTorch was installed under Rosetta emulation (x86_64 pip). 

The torch binary itself is the wrong architecture, and the same issue cascades through transformers and everything else in the venv. The root cause is that I used Xcode's Python 3.9 running under Rosetta, which caused pip to install x86_64 packages instead of native arm64 ones, including PyTorch 2.2.2 instead of the required 2.11.0.

The solution is to completely rebuild the environment: delete the broken venv, install Python 3.14 via Homebrew to get a native arm64 binary, create a fresh venv with that Python version, and reinstall PyTorch 2.11.0 and dependencies at the correct versions.

Now I need to finish the remaining setup steps—logging into HuggingFace and downloading the model—then format all of this as executable directives for another Claude instance to follow.

# COHERE ASR SETUP — REMEDIATION DIRECTIVE
<!-- machine-readable: Claude Code execution guide -->
<!-- context: prior Claude session 40fec642 left ~/cohere_env in a broken state -->
<!-- target: Mac Studio (M2 Ultra, arm64, macOS) -->

## CURRENT BROKEN STATE — DO NOT USE AS-IS

Run this to confirm breakage:
\`\`\`bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python --version
# returns: Python 3.9.6  ← WRONG (needs 3.11+)

~/cohere_env/bin/python -c \"import torch\"
# returns: OSError: libtorch_global_deps.dylib — incompatible architecture (have 'x86_64', need 'arm64')
# ← CRITICAL: entire venv is x86_64, installed under Rosetta emulation
\`\`\`

### Root cause chain
1. Prior Claude used Xcode's bundled Python 3.9 (\`/Applications/Xcode.app/…/Python3.framework\`)
2. That Python was running under Rosetta (x86_64 emulation)
3. pip resolved and downloaded x86_64 torch wheel instead of arm64
4. Installed torch==2.2.2 (wrong version — skill requires torch>=2.11.0)
5. Result: every import of torch throws \`incompatible architecture\` OSError
6. transformers version is also likely wrong — needs >=5.4.0 for CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026

---

## REMEDIATION — EXECUTE IN ORDER

### Step 1: Nuke the broken venv
\`\`\`bash
rm -rf ~/cohere_env
\`\`\`

### Step 2: Verify Homebrew Python is available and arm64
\`\`\`bash
/opt/homebrew/bin/brew install python@3.14
/opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 --version
# expected: Python 3.14.x

arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -c \"import platform; print(platform.machine())\"
# expected: arm64  ← confirm NOT running under Rosetta
\`\`\`

### Step 3: Create a clean arm64 venv at exactly ~/cohere_env
\`\`\`bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -m venv ~/cohere_env
\`\`\`
> CRITICAL: path must be ~/cohere_env exactly — SKILL.md and agents/transcribe-memo.md hardcode this path

### Step 4: Confirm the new venv is native arm64
\`\`\`bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python --version
# expected: Python 3.14.x

~/cohere_env/bin/python -c \"import platform; print(platform.machine())\"
# expected: arm64

cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg
# home = line must point to /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin  NOT /Applications/Xcode.app
\`\`\`
> If pyvenv.cfg shows Xcode Python — STOP, repeat Step 2-3

### Step 5: Install dependencies (correct versions this time)
\`\`\`bash
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip

~/cohere_env/bin/pip install \
  torch>=2.11.0 \
  transformers>=5.4.0 \
  accelerate>=1.13.0 \
  safetensors>=0.7.0 \
  tokenizers>=0.22.2 \
  sentencepiece>=0.2.1 \
  numpy>=2.0.0 \
  librosa>=0.11.0 \
  soundfile>=0.13.0 \
  scipy>=1.17.0 \
  \"huggingface_hub[cli]>=1.8.0\" \
  hf-xet>=1.4.0 \
  tqdm>=4.67.0
\`\`\`

### Step 6: Verify the installs — GATE CHECK
\`\`\`bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c \"
import torch, transformers, numpy, librosa
print('torch:', torch.__version__)           # must be >=2.11.0
print('transformers:', transformers.__version__)  # must be >=5.4.0
print('numpy:', numpy.__version__)            # must be >=2.0.0
print('MPS available:', torch.backends.mps.is_available())  # must be True
print('MPS built:', torch.backends.mps.is_built())          # must be True
\"
\`\`\`
> If MPS is False: machine is M2 Ultra — it MUST be True. Check that python is running as arm64, not under Rosetta.
> If torch < 2.11.0: pip resolved to wrong version — run: pip install \"torch==2.11.0\"

### Step 7: HuggingFace login + model download
\`\`\`bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login
# paste token from https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens (free account is fine)

~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
# ~3 GB — downloads to ~/.cache/huggingface/
# this step is required before first /voice-memos transcription
\`\`\`

### Step 8: Verify skill files are installed correctly
\`\`\`bash
ls ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
# expected: SKILL.md  transcribe_cohere_mps.py  agents/

ls ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
# expected: list-memos.md  transcribe-memo.md  search-memos.md
\`\`\`
> If missing — clone https://github.com/<owner>/voice-memos and run bash setup.sh
> Or manually: cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/

### Step 9: Smoke test
\`\`\`bash
AUDIO_PATH=\"$(ls ~/Library/Group\ Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/*.m4a | head -1)\" \
OUTPUT_PATH=/tmp/test_transcript.txt \
  ~/cohere_env/bin/python ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
# should print: Loading model... then chunk progress... then Done in Xs | RTFx: ~46x
\`\`\`

---

## WHAT PRIOR CLAUDE GOT WRONG — SUMMARY

| Issue | What happened | Correct behavior |
|-------|--------------|-----------------|
| Python version | Used Xcode Python 3.9 from \`/Applications/Xcode.app\` | Use Homebrew Python >=3.11 from \`/opt/homebrew\` |
| Architecture | pip ran under Rosetta, downloaded x86_64 wheels | Run \`arch -arm64\` to force native; verify with \`platform.machine()\` |
| PyTorch version | Installed 2.2.2 after declaring 2.11.0 \"doesn't exist\" without checking | Always check PyPI/internet FIRST; torch 2.11.0 was released March 2026 |
| torch dylib arch | x86_64 binary on arm64 machine — hard OSError crash on import | arm64 venv + arm64 Python = arm64 wheels automatically |
| HF model | Never downloaded; login step skipped | \`huggingface-cli login\` + \`huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026\` |
| venv path | May have been created in wrong location during session | MUST be exactly \`~/cohere_env\` — SKILL.md hardcodes this path |

---

## REFERENCE — WORKING STATE (macbook-pro)
The working reference machine (macbook-pro, Tailscale 100.85.240.55) has:
- Python 3.14.3 from \`/opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14\`

content.content:
1	# CLAUDE.md - Voice Memos Skill Setup Protocol for AI Assistants
2	
3	This document provides comprehensive setup guidance for Claude Code and other AI assistants setting up the voice-memos skill. It addresses critical pitfalls that can cause cascade failures during installation.
4	
5	## ⚠️ Critical Architecture Issue
6	
7	**The primary failure pattern**: x86_64 Homebrew on Apple Silicon Macs leads to wrong Python → wrong packages → MPS unavailable → transcription failure.
8	
9	Always verify native arm64 execution before proceeding.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Setup Protocol
14	
15	### Phase 1: Architecture Detection & Validation
16	
17	**GATE CHECK 1: Verify Apple Silicon Hardware**
18	```bash
19	sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string
20	# MUST show: "Apple M1" / "Apple M2" / "Apple M3" / "Apple M4" (any variant)
21	```
22	
23	**GATE CHECK 2: Detect Current Execution Mode**
24	```bash
25	arch
26	uname -m
27	```
28	- If both show `arm64` → ✅ Native execution
29	- If either shows `i386` or `x86_64` → ⚠️ Running under Rosetta emulation
30	
31	**GATE CHECK 3: Homebrew Architecture Detection**
32	```bash
33	# Check for native arm64 Homebrew
34	ls /opt/homebrew/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "✅ Native Homebrew found" || echo "❌ Native Homebrew missing"
35	
36	# Check for x86_64 Homebrew
37	ls /usr/local/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "⚠️ x86_64 Homebrew found" || echo "✅ No x86_64 Homebrew"
38	
39	# Verify which brew is in PATH
40	which brew
41	```
42	
43	**Expected Results:**
44	- ✅ Native: `/opt/homebrew/bin/brew` exists and is in PATH
45	- ❌ Problem: Only `/usr/local/bin/brew` exists (x86_64 Homebrew)
46	
47	### Phase 2: Homebrew Installation/Verification
48	
49	**If native Homebrew missing:**
50	```bash
51	# Install native arm64 Homebrew (requires user sudo)
52	arch -arm64 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
53	
54	# Add to PATH
55	echo >> ~/.zprofile
56	echo 'eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"' >> ~/.zprofile
57	eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"
58	```
59	
60	**GATE CHECK 4: Homebrew Validation**
61	```bash
62	arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew --version
63	# MUST work without "Bad CPU type" error
64	```
65	
66	### Phase 3: Python Installation & Verification
67	
68	**Install Python 3.14+ via native Homebrew:**
69	```bash
70	arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew install python@3.14
71	```
72	
73	**GATE CHECK 5: Python Architecture Validation**
74	```bash
75	arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
76	# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"
77	```
78	
79	### Phase 4: Virtual Environment Setup
80	
81	**Create native arm64 venv:**
82	```bash
83	arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -m venv ~/cohere_env
84	```
85	
86	**CRITICAL: Fix venv symlinks (common failure point)**
87	```bash
88	# Verify venv configuration
89	~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(platform.machine())"
90	cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home
91	
92	# If python reports x86_64, fix symlinks:
93	cd ~/cohere_env/bin
94	rm python python3 python3.9 2>/dev/null  # Remove wrong symlinks
95	ln -s python3.14 python3
96	ln -s python3.14 python
97	```
98	
99	**GATE CHECK 6: Virtual Environment Validation**
100	```bash
101	~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
102	# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"
103	
104	# Verify venv points to correct Python
105	cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home
106	# MUST show: home = /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin
107	```
108	
109	### Phase 5: Package Installation
110	
111	**Install dependencies with correct architecture:**
112	```bash
113	~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip
114	
115	~/cohere_env/bin/pip install \
116	  'torch>=2.11.0' \
117	  'transformers>=5.4.0' \
118	  'accelerate>=1.13.0' \
119	  'safetensors>=0.7.0' \
120	  'tokenizers>=0.22.2' \
121	  'sentencepiece>=0.2.1' \
122	  'numpy>=2.0.0' \
123	  'librosa>=0.11.0' \
124	  'soundfile>=0.13.0' \
125	  'scipy>=1.17.0' \
126	  'huggingface_hub[cli]>=1.8.0' \
127	  'hf-xet>=1.4.0' \
128	  'tqdm>=4.67.0'
129	```
130	
131	**GATE CHECK 7: Package and MPS Validation**
132	```bash
133	~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "
134	import torch, transformers, numpy, librosa
135	print('✅ torch:', torch.__version__)
136	print('✅ transformers:', transformers.__version__)
137	print('✅ numpy:', numpy.__version__)
138	print('✅ MPS available:', torch.backends.mps.is_available())
139	print('✅ MPS built:', torch.backends.mps.is_built())
140	"
141	```
142	
143	**Required output:**
144	- torch: 2.11.0 or higher
145	- transformers: 5.4.0 or higher
146	- numpy: 2.0.0 or higher
147	- MPS available: True
148	- MPS built: True
149	
150	### Phase 6: Model Download
151	
152	**HuggingFace setup:**
153	```bash
154	# Use system huggingface-cli if available, or install in venv
155	which huggingface-cli || ~/cohere_env/bin/pip install huggingface_hub[cli]
156	
157	# Download model (may require HF login)
158	/usr/local/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
159	# OR: ~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
160	```
161	
162	### Phase 7: Skill Installation
163	
164	**Copy skill files to Claude Code directory:**
165	```bash
166	mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents
167	cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
168	cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
169	chmod +x ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
170	```
171	
172	**GATE CHECK 8: Smoke Test**
173	```bash
174	AUDIO_PATH="/Users/kairi/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/20220107 163459-D1CE465C.m4a" \
175	OUTPUT_PATH=/tmp/test_transcript.txt \
176	~/cohere_env/bin/python ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
177	```
178	
179	Expected: Model loads → transcribes → "RTFx: ~65x"
180	
181	---
182	
183	## Common Failure Patterns & Solutions
184	
185	### Error: "Bad CPU type in executable"
186	**Cause:** x86_64 binary on arm64 system
187	**Solution:** Use `arch -arm64` prefix and native Homebrew
188	
189	### Error: "MPS not available" (MPS built: False)
190	**Cause:** PyTorch installed for wrong architecture
191	**Solution:** Recreate venv with native Python, reinstall torch
192	
193	### Error: "value cannot be converted to type c10::Half without overflow"
194	**Cause:** Model architecture mismatch (red herring - real issue is wrong PyTorch)
195	**Solution:** Fix underlying architecture issues, not the model code
196	
197	### Error: Virtual environment shows wrong Python version
198	**Cause:** Broken symlinks in venv/bin/
199	**Solution:** Fix symlinks to point to correct python3.14
200	
201	### Error: "Permission denied" during Homebrew install
202	**Cause:** Needs sudo access
203	**Solution:** Ask user to run installation command manually
204	
205	### Error: "sqlite3: unable to open database"
206	**Cause:** Terminal lacks Full Disk Access permission
207	**Solution:** System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access
208	
209	---
210	
211	## Working Reference Configuration
212	
213	**Known-good setup (macbook-pro reference):**
214	- Hardware: Apple M2 Ultra
215	- Python: 3.14.3 from `/opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14`
216	- Homebrew: Native arm64 at `/opt/homebrew`

218	- Virtual env: `~/cohere_env` with correct symlinks
219	- Model cached: `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--CohereLabs--cohere-transcribe-03-2026/`
220	
221	**Validation commands for working state:**

225	~/cohere_env/bin/python --version     # → Python 3.14.3
226	~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"  # → True
227	```
228	
229	---
230	
231	## Emergency Remediation

235	# 1. Remove broken environment
236	rm -rf ~/cohere_env
237	
238	# 2. Install native Homebrew (ask user for sudo)

240	
241	# 3. Follow setup protocol from Phase 2
242	```
243

245	1. Document existing packages: `brew list --formula > /tmp/x86_packages.txt`
246	2. Install native Homebrew
247	3. Reinstall packages with native brew
248	4. Remove old Homebrew: `sudo rm -rf /usr/local/Homebrew`
249	
250	---

# CLAUDE.md - Voice Memos Skill Setup Protocol for AI Assistants

This document provides comprehensive setup guidance for Claude Code and other AI assistants setting up the voice-memos skill. It addresses critical pitfalls that can cause cascade failures during installation.

## ⚠️ Critical Architecture Issue

**The primary failure pattern**: x86_64 Homebrew on Apple Silicon Macs leads to wrong Python → wrong packages → MPS unavailable → transcription failure.

Always verify native arm64 execution before proceeding.

---

## Setup Protocol

### Phase 1: Architecture Detection & Validation

**GATE CHECK 1: Verify Apple Silicon Hardware**
```bash
sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string
# MUST show: "Apple M1" / "Apple M2" / "Apple M3" / "Apple M4" (any variant)
```

**GATE CHECK 2: Detect Current Execution Mode**
```bash
arch
uname -m
```
- If both show `arm64` → ✅ Native execution
- If either shows `i386` or `x86_64` → ⚠️ Running under Rosetta emulation

**GATE CHECK 3: Homebrew Architecture Detection**
```bash
# Check for native arm64 Homebrew
ls /opt/homebrew/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "✅ Native Homebrew found" || echo "❌ Native Homebrew missing"

# Check for x86_64 Homebrew
ls /usr/local/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "⚠️ x86_64 Homebrew found" || echo "✅ No x86_64 Homebrew"

# Verify which brew is in PATH
which brew
```

**Expected Results:**
- ✅ Native: `/opt/homebrew/bin/brew` exists and is in PATH
- ❌ Problem: Only `/usr/local/bin/brew` exists (x86_64 Homebrew)

### Phase 2: Homebrew Installation/Verification

**If native Homebrew missing:**
```bash
# Install native arm64 Homebrew (requires user sudo)
arch -arm64 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Add to PATH
echo >> ~/.zprofile
echo 'eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"' >> ~/.zprofile
eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"
```

**GATE CHECK 4: Homebrew Validation**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew --version
# MUST work without "Bad CPU type" error
```

### Phase 3: Python Installation & Verification

**Install Python 3.14+ via native Homebrew:**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew install python@3.14
```

**GATE CHECK 5: Python Architecture Validation**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"
```

### Phase 4: Virtual Environment Setup

**Create native arm64 venv:**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -m venv ~/cohere_env
```

**CRITICAL: Fix venv symlinks (common failure point)**
```bash
# Verify venv configuration
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(platform.machine())"
cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home

# If python reports x86_64, fix symlinks:
cd ~/cohere_env/bin
rm python python3 python3.9 2>/dev/null  # Remove wrong symlinks
ln -s python3.14 python3
ln -s python3.14 python
```

**GATE CHECK 6: Virtual Environment Validation**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"

# Verify venv points to correct Python
cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home
# MUST show: home = /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin
```

### Phase 5: Package Installation

**Install dependencies with correct architecture:**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip

~/cohere_env/bin/pip install \
  'torch>=2.11.0' \
  'transformers>=5.4.0' \
  'accelerate>=1.13.0' \
  'safetensors>=0.7.0' \
  'tokenizers>=0.22.2' \
  'sentencepiece>=0.2.1' \
  'numpy>=2.0.0' \
  'librosa>=0.11.0' \
  'soundfile>=0.13.0' \
  'scipy>=1.17.0' \
  'huggingface_hub[cli]>=1.8.0' \
  'hf-xet>=1.4.0' \
  'tqdm>=4.67.0'
```

**GATE CHECK 7: Package and MPS Validation**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "
import torch, transformers, numpy, librosa
print('✅ torch:', torch.__version__)
print('✅ transformers:', transformers.__version__)
print('✅ numpy:', numpy.__version__)
print('✅ MPS available:', torch.backends.mps.is_available())
print('✅ MPS built:', torch.backends.mps.is_built())
"
```

**Required output:**
- torch: 2.11.0 or higher
- transformers: 5.4.0 or higher
- numpy: 2.0.0 or higher
- MPS available: True
- MPS built: True

### Phase 6: Model Download

**HuggingFace setup:**
```bash
# Use system huggingface-cli if available, or install in venv
which huggingface-cli || ~/cohere_env/bin/pip install huggingface_hub[cli]

# Download model (may require HF login)
/usr/local/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
# OR: ~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
```

### Phase 7: Skill Installation

**Copy skill files to Claude Code directory:**
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents
cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
chmod +x ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

**GATE CHECK 8: Smoke Test**
```bash
AUDIO_PATH="/Users/kairi/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/20220107 163459-D1CE465C.m4a" \
OUTPUT_PATH=/tmp/test_transcript.txt \
~/cohere_env/bin/python ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

Expected: Model loads → transcribes → "RTFx: ~65x"

---

## Common Failure Patterns & Solutions

### Error: "Bad CPU type in executable"
**Cause:** x86_64 binary on arm64 system
**Solution:** Use `arch -arm64` prefix and native Homebrew

### Error: "MPS not available" (MPS built: False)
**Cause:** PyTorch installed for wrong architecture
**Solution:** Recreate venv with native Python, reinstall torch

### Error: "value cannot be converted to type c10::Half without overflow"
**Cause:** Model architecture mismatch (red herring - real issue is wrong PyTorch)
**Solution:** Fix underlying architecture issues, not the model code

### Error: Virtual environment shows wrong Python version
**Cause:** Broken symlinks in venv/bin/
**Solution:** Fix symlinks to point to correct python3.14

### Error: "Permission denied" during Homebrew install
**Cause:** Needs sudo access
**Solution:** Ask user to run installation command manually

### Error: "sqlite3: unable to open database"
**Cause:** Terminal lacks Full Disk Access permission
**Solution:** System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

---

## Working Reference Configuration

**Known-good setup (macbook-pro reference):**
- Hardware: Apple M2 Ultra
- Python: 3.14.3 from `/opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14`
- Homebrew: Native arm64 at `/opt/homebrew`
- PyTorch: 2.11.0 (arm64 native)
- Virtual env: `~/cohere_env` with correct symlinks
- Model cached: `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--CohereLabs--cohere-transcribe-03-2026/`

**Validation commands for working state:**
```bash
arch                                    # → arm64
which brew                             # → /opt/homebrew/bin/brew
~/cohere_env/bin/python --version     # → Python 3.14.3
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"  # → True
```

---

## Emergency Remediation

**If everything is broken, nuclear reset:**
```bash
# 1. Remove broken environment
rm -rf ~/cohere_env

# 2. Install native Homebrew (ask user for sudo)
arch -arm64 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# 3. Follow setup protocol from Phase 2
```

**Migration from x86_64 to arm64 Homebrew:**
1. Document existing packages: `brew list --formula > /tmp/x86_packages.txt`
2. Install native Homebrew
3. Reinstall packages with native brew
4. Remove old Homebrew: `sudo rm -rf /usr/local/Homebrew`

---

## Key Success Principles

1. **Always verify architecture** before proceeding
2. **Use `arch -arm64` prefix** for critical commands
3. **Validate at each phase** before continuing
4. **Fix symlinks** in virtual environments
5. **Don't modify original skill files** - use them as-is
6. **When in doubt, start fresh** with native tools

This protocol prevents the cascade failures that occur when wrong architecture components interact with each other.

# voice-memos

A [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) skill that transcribes and summarizes Apple Voice Memos using your Mac's Apple Silicon GPU.

> **Apple Silicon Macs only (M1 / M2 / M3 / M4).** Uses PyTorch MPS backend for GPU inference.

## What it does

| Command | Result |
|---|---|
| `/voice-memos` | Lists all your Voice Memos |
| `/voice-memos <title>` | Transcribes + summarizes that memo |
| `/voice-memos <title> --qa` | Adds Q&A highlights section |
| `/voice-memos <title> --speakers "Alice (host), Bob (guest)"` | Speaker-attributed transcript |
| `/voice-memos search <keyword>` | Searches across memos |

Follow-up questions work naturally — the transcript is cached so nothing gets re-transcribed.

## Requirements

- **Apple Silicon Mac** (M1, M2, M3, or M4)
- **macOS 13+**
- **Full Disk Access** for Terminal: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

## Installation

Clone this repo and let Claude Code handle the setup:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/voice-memos.git
cd voice-memos
```

Then in Claude Code:
```
Set up the voice-memos skill from the current directory
```

## Performance

~65× real-time speed on Apple Silicon with MPS:

| Recording length | Transcription time |
|---|---|
| 5 minutes | ~5 seconds |
| 15 minutes | ~14 seconds |
| 30 minutes | ~27 seconds |
| 60 minutes | ~55 seconds |

## Privacy

All processing is local. Your audio never leaves your machine.

## License

MIT

### Phase 5: Package Installation

**Install dependencies with correct architecture:**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip

~/cohere_env/bin/pip install \
  'torch>=2.11.0' \
  'transformers>=5.4.0' \
  'accelerate>=1.13.0' \
  'safetensors>=0.7.0' \
  'tokenizers>=0.22.2' \
  'sentencepiece>=0.2.1' \
  'numpy>=2.0.0' \
  'librosa>=0.11.0' \
  'soundfile>=0.13.0' \
  'scipy>=1.17.0' \
  'huggingface_hub[cli]>=1.8.0' \
  'hf-xet>=1.4.0' \
  'tqdm>=4.67.0'
```

**GATE CHECK 7: Package and MPS Validation**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "
import torch, transformers, numpy, librosa
print('✅ torch:', torch.__version__)
print('✅ transformers:', transformers.__version__)
print('✅ numpy:', numpy.__version__)
print('✅ MPS available:', torch.backends.mps.is_available())
print('✅ MPS built:', torch.backends.mps.is_built())
"
```

**Required output:**
- torch: 2.11.0 or higher
- transformers: 5.4.0 or higher
- numpy: 2.0.0 or higher
- MPS available: True
- MPS built: True

### Phase 6: Model Download

**HuggingFace setup:**
```bash
# Use system huggingface-cli if available, or install in venv
which huggingface-cli || ~/cohere_env/bin/pip install huggingface_hub[cli]

# Download model (may require HF login)
/usr/local/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
# OR: ~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
```

### Phase 7: Skill Installation

**Copy skill files to Claude Code directory:**
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents
cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
chmod +x ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

**GATE CHECK 8: Smoke Test**
```bash
AUDIO_PATH="/Users/kairi/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/20220107 163459-D1CE465C.m4a" \
OUTPUT_PATH=/tmp/test_transcript.txt \
~/cohere_env/bin/python ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

Expected: Model loads → transcribes → "RTFx: ~65x"

---

## Common Failure Patterns & Solutions

### Error: "Bad CPU type in executable"
**Cause:** x86_64 binary on arm64 system
**Solution:** Use `arch -arm64` prefix and native Homebrew

### Error: "MPS not available" (MPS built: False)
**Cause:** PyTorch installed for wrong architecture
**Solution:** Recreate venv with native Python, reinstall torch

### Error: "value cannot be converted to type c10::Half without overflow"
**Cause:** Model architecture mismatch (red herring - real issue is wrong PyTorch)
**Solution:** Fix underlying architecture issues, not the model code

### Error: Virtual environment shows wrong Python version
**Cause:** Broken symlinks in venv/bin/
**Solution:** Fix symlinks to point to correct python3.14

### Error: "Permission denied" during Homebrew install
**Cause:** Needs sudo access
**Solution:** Ask user to run installation command manually

### Error: "sqlite3: unable to open database"
**Cause:** Terminal lacks Full Disk Access permission
**Solution:** System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

---

## Working Reference Configuration

**Known-good setup (macbook-pro reference):**
- Hardware: Apple M2 Ultra
- Python: 3.14.3 from `/opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14`
- Homebrew: Native arm64 at `/opt/homebrew`
- PyTorch: 2.11.0 (arm64 native)
- Virtual env: `~/cohere_env` with correct symlinks
- Model cached: `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--CohereLabs--cohere-transcribe-03-2026/`

**Validation commands for working state:**
```bash
arch                                    # → arm64
which brew                             # → /opt/homebrew/bin/brew
~/cohere_env/bin/python --version     # → Python 3.14.3
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"  # → True
```

---

## Emergency Remediation

**If everything is broken, nuclear reset:**
```bash
# 1. Remove broken environment
rm -rf ~/cohere_env

# 2. Install native Homebrew (ask user for sudo)
arch -arm64 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# 3. Follow setup protocol from Phase 2
```

**Migration from x86_64 to arm64 Homebrew:**
1. Document existing packages: `brew list --formula > /tmp/x86_packages.txt`
2. Install native Homebrew
3. Reinstall packages with native brew
4. Remove old Homebrew: `sudo rm -rf /usr/local/Homebrew`

---

## Key Success Principles

1. **Always verify architecture** before proceeding
2. **Use `arch -arm64` prefix** for critical commands
3. **Validate at each phase** before continuing
4. **Fix symlinks** in virtual environments
5. **Don't modify original skill files** - use them as-is
6. **When in doubt, start fresh** with native tools

This protocol prevents the cascade failures that occur when wrong architecture components interact with each other.

# CLAUDE.md - Voice Memos Skill Setup Protocol for AI Assistants

This document provides comprehensive setup guidance for Claude Code and other AI assistants setting up the voice-memos skill. It addresses critical pitfalls that can cause cascade failures during installation.

## ⚠️ Critical Architecture Issue

**The primary failure pattern**: x86_64 Homebrew on Apple Silicon Macs leads to wrong Python → wrong packages → MPS unavailable → transcription failure.

Always verify native arm64 execution before proceeding.

---

## Setup Protocol

### Phase 1: Architecture Detection & Validation

**GATE CHECK 1: Verify Apple Silicon Hardware**
```bash
sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string
# MUST show: "Apple M1" / "Apple M2" / "Apple M3" / "Apple M4" (any variant)
```

**GATE CHECK 2: Detect Current Execution Mode**
```bash
arch
uname -m
```
- If both show `arm64` → ✅ Native execution
- If either shows `i386` or `x86_64` → ⚠️ Running under Rosetta emulation

**GATE CHECK 3: Homebrew Architecture Detection**
```bash
# Check for native arm64 Homebrew
ls /opt/homebrew/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "✅ Native Homebrew found" || echo "❌ Native Homebrew missing"

# Check for x86_64 Homebrew
ls /usr/local/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "⚠️ x86_64 Homebrew found" || echo "✅ No x86_64 Homebrew"

# Verify which brew is in PATH
which brew
```

**Expected Results:**
- ✅ Native: `/opt/homebrew/bin/brew` exists and is in PATH
- ❌ Problem: Only `/usr/local/bin/brew` exists (x86_64 Homebrew)

### Phase 2: Homebrew Installation/Verification

**If native Homebrew missing:**
```bash
# Install native arm64 Homebrew (requires user sudo)
arch -arm64 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Add to PATH
echo >> ~/.zprofile
echo 'eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"' >> ~/.zprofile
eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"
```

**GATE CHECK 4: Homebrew Validation**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew --version
# MUST work without "Bad CPU type" error
```

### Phase 3: Python Installation & Verification

**Install Python 3.14+ via native Homebrew:**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew install python@3.14
```

**GATE CHECK 5: Python Architecture Validation**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"
```

### Phase 4: Virtual Environment Setup

**Create native arm64 venv:**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -m venv ~/cohere_env
```

**CRITICAL: Fix venv symlinks (common failure point)**
```bash
# Verify venv configuration
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(platform.machine())"
cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home

# If python reports x86_64, fix symlinks:
cd ~/cohere_env/bin
rm python python3 python3.9 2>/dev/null  # Remove wrong symlinks
ln -s python3.14 python3
ln -s python3.14 python
```

**GATE CHECK 6: Virtual Environment Validation**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"

# Verify venv points to correct Python
cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home
# MUST show: home = /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin
```

### Phase 5: Package Installation

**Install dependencies with correct architecture:**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip

~/cohere_env/bin/pip install \
  'torch>=2.11.0' \
  'transformers>=5.4.0' \
  'accelerate>=1.13.0' \
  'safetensors>=0.7.0' \
  'tokenizers>=0.22.2' \
  'sentencepiece>=0.2.1' \
  'numpy>=2.0.0' \
  'librosa>=0.11.0' \
  'soundfile>=0.13.0' \
  'scipy>=1.17.0' \
  'huggingface_hub[cli]>=1.8.0' \
  'hf-xet>=1.4.0' \
  'tqdm>=4.67.0'
```

**GATE CHECK 7: Package and MPS Validation**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "
import torch, transformers, numpy, librosa
print('✅ torch:', torch.__version__)
print('✅ transformers:', transformers.__version__)
print('✅ numpy:', numpy.__version__)
print('✅ MPS available:', torch.backends.mps.is_available())
print('✅ MPS built:', torch.backends.mps.is_built())
"
```

**Required output:**
- torch: 2.11.0 or higher
- transformers: 5.4.0 or higher
- numpy: 2.0.0 or higher
- MPS available: True
- MPS built: True

### Phase 6: Model Download

**HuggingFace setup:**
```bash
# Use system huggingface-cli if available, or install in venv
which huggingface-cli || ~/cohere_env/bin/pip install huggingface_hub[cli]

# Download model (may require HF login)
/usr/local/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
# OR: ~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
```

### Phase 7: Skill Installation

**Copy skill files to Claude Code directory:**
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents
cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
chmod +x ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

**GATE CHECK 8: Smoke Test**
```bash
AUDIO_PATH="/Users/kairi/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/20220107 163459-D1CE465C.m4a" \
OUTPUT_PATH=/tmp/test_transcript.txt \
~/cohere_env/bin/python ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

Expected: Model loads → transcribes → "RTFx: ~65x"

---

## Common Failure Patterns & Solutions

**Cause:** x86_64 binary on arm64 system

**Solution:** System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

---

## Working Reference Configuration

**Known-good setup (macbook-pro reference):**

- Homebrew: Native arm64 at `/opt/homebrew`

content.content:
# voice-memos

A [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) skill that transcribes and summarizes Apple Voice Memos using your Mac's Apple Silicon GPU.

> **Apple Silicon Macs only (M1 / M2 / M3 / M4).** Uses PyTorch MPS backend for GPU inference.

## What it does

| Command | Result |
|---|---|
| `/voice-memos` | Lists all your Voice Memos |
| `/voice-memos <title>` | Transcribes + summarizes that memo |
| `/voice-memos <title> --qa` | Adds Q&A highlights section |
| `/voice-memos <title> --speakers "Alice (host), Bob (guest)"` | Speaker-attributed transcript |
| `/voice-memos search <keyword>` | Searches across memos |

Follow-up questions work naturally — the transcript is cached so nothing gets re-transcribed.

## Requirements

- **Apple Silicon Mac** (M1, M2, M3, or M4)
- **macOS 13+**
- **Full Disk Access** for Terminal: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

## Installation

Clone this repo and let Claude Code handle the setup:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/voice-memos.git
cd voice-memos
```

Then in Claude Code:
```
Set up the voice-memos skill from the current directory
```

## Performance

~65× real-time speed on Apple Silicon with MPS:

| Recording length | Transcription time |
|---|---|
| 5 minutes | ~5 seconds |
| 15 minutes | ~14 seconds |
| 30 minutes | ~27 seconds |
| 60 minutes | ~55 seconds |

## Privacy

All processing is local. Your audio never leaves your machine.

## License

MIT

## Requirements

- **Apple Silicon Mac** (M1, M2, M3, or M4)
- **macOS 13+**
- **Full Disk Access** for Terminal: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access
- **HuggingFace account** (free) — required to download the transcription model
- **Gated model access accepted** — you must visit the [CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026](https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026) model page and click **"Agree and access repository"** to share your HF username with Cohere. Without this, the download returns a 403 even with a valid token.
- **`huggingface-cli` installed and authenticated** before setup runs any download

## Installation

### Before you begin — HuggingFace setup (required)

1. Create a free account at [huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co/join) if you don't have one
2. Visit [CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026](https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026) and click **"Agree and access repository"** to accept the model's terms and share your HF username with Cohere
3. Create an access token at [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens)
4. Install and authenticate the CLI:
   ```bash
   pip install huggingface_hub[cli]   # or use the venv after setup.sh creates it
   huggingface-cli login              # paste your token when prompted
   ```

> If you skip any of these steps, the model download will fail and the skill won't be able to transcribe.

### Clone and run setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/voice-memos.git
cd voice-memos
bash setup.sh
```

`setup.sh` will create the Python venv, install all dependencies, and copy the skill files into `~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/`. It will also remind you to log in if the CLI isn't authenticated.

Then in Claude Code:
```
/voice-memos
```

# voice-memos

A [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) skill that transcribes and summarizes Apple Voice Memos using your Mac's Apple Silicon GPU.

> **Apple Silicon Macs only (M1 / M2 / M3 / M4).** Uses PyTorch MPS backend for GPU inference.

## What it does

| Command | Result |
|---|---|
| `/voice-memos` | Lists all your Voice Memos |
| `/voice-memos <title>` | Transcribes + summarizes that memo |
| `/voice-memos <title> --qa` | Adds Q&A highlights section |
| `/voice-memos <title> --speakers "Alice (host), Bob (guest)"` | Speaker-attributed transcript |
| `/voice-memos search <keyword>` | Searches across memos |

Follow-up questions work naturally — the transcript is cached so nothing gets re-transcribed.

## Requirements

- **Apple Silicon Mac** (M1, M2, M3, or M4)
- **macOS 13+**
- **Full Disk Access** for Terminal: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

## Installation

Clone this repo and let Claude Code handle the setup:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/voice-memos.git
cd voice-memos
```

Then in Claude Code:
```
Set up the voice-memos skill from the current directory
```

## Performance

~65× real-time speed on Apple Silicon with MPS:

| Recording length | Transcription time |
|---|---|
| 5 minutes | ~5 seconds |
| 15 minutes | ~14 seconds |
| 30 minutes | ~27 seconds |
| 60 minutes | ~55 seconds |

## Privacy

All processing is local. Your audio never leaves your machine.

## License

MIT

### Phase 6: Model Download

**HuggingFace setup:**
```bash
# Use system huggingface-cli if available, or install in venv
which huggingface-cli || ~/cohere_env/bin/pip install huggingface_hub[cli]

input.new_string:
### Phase 6: HuggingFace Account, Gated Access, and Model Download

This phase has three hard prerequisites that must ALL be satisfied before the download command will succeed. A 403 error means one of them is missing.

**PREREQUISITE 1 — Active HuggingFace account**
The user must have a registered account at huggingface.co. This is free. Cannot be automated — the user must do this manually if they haven't already.

**PREREQUISITE 2 — Accept gated model access (cannot be skipped)**
`CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026` is a gated model. The user must visit the model page in a browser and click "Agree and access repository". This shares their HF username with Cohere and grants download permission.
```
URL: https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
Action: Click "Agree and access repository"
```
Without this step, the download returns HTTP 403 even with a valid token. There is no CLI workaround for this — it must be done in the browser.

**PREREQUISITE 3 — huggingface-cli installed and authenticated**
Always use the venv's CLI, not a system-level one (system path varies and may be absent):
```bash
# Verify CLI is installed in venv (it is, from Phase 5's huggingface_hub[cli])
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli --version

# Authenticate — user pastes their token from huggingface.co/settings/tokens
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli login
```

**GATE CHECK: confirm login succeeded**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli whoami
# Must return a username — NOT "Not logged in"
```

**Download the model (~3 GB, cached to ~/.cache/huggingface/)**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
```
Expected: progress bars to 100%, then "Successfully downloaded model to ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--CohereLabs--cohere-transcribe-03-2026/"

If download fails with 403: user has not completed PREREQUISITE 2. Direct them to the model page URL above.

# CLAUDE.md - Voice Memos Skill Setup Protocol for AI Assistants

This document provides comprehensive setup guidance for Claude Code and other AI assistants setting up the voice-memos skill. It addresses critical pitfalls that can cause cascade failures during installation.

## ⚠️ Critical Architecture Issue

**The primary failure pattern**: x86_64 Homebrew on Apple Silicon Macs leads to wrong Python → wrong packages → MPS unavailable → transcription failure.

Always verify native arm64 execution before proceeding.

---

## Setup Protocol

### Phase 1: Architecture Detection & Validation

**GATE CHECK 1: Verify Apple Silicon Hardware**
```bash
sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string
# MUST show: "Apple M1" / "Apple M2" / "Apple M3" / "Apple M4" (any variant)
```

**GATE CHECK 2: Detect Current Execution Mode**
```bash
arch
uname -m
```
- If both show `arm64` → ✅ Native execution
- If either shows `i386` or `x86_64` → ⚠️ Running under Rosetta emulation

**GATE CHECK 3: Homebrew Architecture Detection**
```bash
# Check for native arm64 Homebrew
ls /opt/homebrew/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "✅ Native Homebrew found" || echo "❌ Native Homebrew missing"

# Check for x86_64 Homebrew
ls /usr/local/bin/brew 2>/dev/null && echo "⚠️ x86_64 Homebrew found" || echo "✅ No x86_64 Homebrew"

# Verify which brew is in PATH
which brew
```

**Expected Results:**
- ✅ Native: `/opt/homebrew/bin/brew` exists and is in PATH
- ❌ Problem: Only `/usr/local/bin/brew` exists (x86_64 Homebrew)

### Phase 2: Homebrew Installation/Verification

**If native Homebrew missing:**
```bash
# Install native arm64 Homebrew (requires user sudo)
arch -arm64 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Add to PATH
echo >> ~/.zprofile
echo 'eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"' >> ~/.zprofile
eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv zsh)"
```

**GATE CHECK 4: Homebrew Validation**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew --version
# MUST work without "Bad CPU type" error
```

### Phase 3: Python Installation & Verification

**Install Python 3.14+ via native Homebrew:**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew install python@3.14
```

**GATE CHECK 5: Python Architecture Validation**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"
```

### Phase 4: Virtual Environment Setup

**Create native arm64 venv:**
```bash
arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin/python3.14 -m venv ~/cohere_env
```

**CRITICAL: Fix venv symlinks (common failure point)**
```bash
# Verify venv configuration
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(platform.machine())"
cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home

# If python reports x86_64, fix symlinks:
cd ~/cohere_env/bin
rm python python3 python3.9 2>/dev/null  # Remove wrong symlinks
ln -s python3.14 python3
ln -s python3.14 python
```

**GATE CHECK 6: Virtual Environment Validation**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import platform; print(f'Python {platform.python_version()} - {platform.machine()}')"
# MUST show: "Python 3.14.x - arm64"

# Verify venv points to correct Python
cat ~/cohere_env/pyvenv.cfg | grep home
# MUST show: home = /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14/bin
```

### Phase 5: Package Installation

**Install dependencies with correct architecture:**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip

~/cohere_env/bin/pip install \
  'torch>=2.11.0' \
  'transformers>=5.4.0' \
  'accelerate>=1.13.0' \
  'safetensors>=0.7.0' \
  'tokenizers>=0.22.2' \
  'sentencepiece>=0.2.1' \
  'numpy>=2.0.0' \
  'librosa>=0.11.0' \
  'soundfile>=0.13.0' \
  'scipy>=1.17.0' \
  'huggingface_hub[cli]>=1.8.0' \
  'hf-xet>=1.4.0' \
  'tqdm>=4.67.0'
```

**GATE CHECK 7: Package and MPS Validation**
```bash
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "
import torch, transformers, numpy, librosa
print('✅ torch:', torch.__version__)
print('✅ transformers:', transformers.__version__)
print('✅ numpy:', numpy.__version__)
print('✅ MPS available:', torch.backends.mps.is_available())
print('✅ MPS built:', torch.backends.mps.is_built())
"
```

**Required output:**
- torch: 2.11.0 or higher
- transformers: 5.4.0 or higher
- numpy: 2.0.0 or higher
- MPS available: True
- MPS built: True

### Phase 6: Model Download

**HuggingFace setup:**
```bash
# Use system huggingface-cli if available, or install in venv
which huggingface-cli || ~/cohere_env/bin/pip install huggingface_hub[cli]

# Download model (may require HF login)
/usr/local/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
# OR: ~/cohere_env/bin/huggingface-cli download CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026
```

### Phase 7: Skill Installation

**Copy skill files to Claude Code directory:**
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents
cp SKILL.md transcribe_cohere_mps.py ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/
cp agents/*.md ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/agents/
chmod +x ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

**GATE CHECK 8: Smoke Test**
```bash
AUDIO_PATH="/Users/kairi/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.VoiceMemos.shared/Recordings/20220107 163459-D1CE465C.m4a" \
OUTPUT_PATH=/tmp/test_transcript.txt \
~/cohere_env/bin/python ~/.claude/skills/voice-memos/transcribe_cohere_mps.py
```

Expected: Model loads → transcribes → "RTFx: ~65x"

---

## Common Failure Patterns & Solutions

### Error: "Bad CPU type in executable"
**Cause:** x86_64 binary on arm64 system
**Solution:** Use `arch -arm64` prefix and native Homebrew

### Error: "MPS not available" (MPS built: False)
**Cause:** PyTorch installed for wrong architecture
**Solution:** Recreate venv with native Python, reinstall torch

### Error: "value cannot be converted to type c10::Half without overflow"
**Cause:** Model architecture mismatch (red herring - real issue is wrong PyTorch)
**Solution:** Fix underlying architecture issues, not the model code

### Error: Virtual environment shows wrong Python version
**Cause:** Broken symlinks in venv/bin/
**Solution:** Fix symlinks to point to correct python3.14

### Error: "Permission denied" during Homebrew install
**Cause:** Needs sudo access
**Solution:** Ask user to run installation command manually

### Error: "sqlite3: unable to open database"
**Cause:** Terminal lacks Full Disk Access permission
**Solution:** System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

---

## Working Reference Configuration

**Known-good setup (macbook-pro reference):**
- Hardware: Apple M2 Ultra
- Python: 3.14.3 from `/opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.14`
- Homebrew: Native arm64 at `/opt/homebrew`
- PyTorch: 2.11.0 (arm64 native)
- Virtual env: `~/cohere_env` with correct symlinks
- Model cached: `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--CohereLabs--cohere-transcribe-03-2026/`

**Validation commands for working state:**
```bash
arch                                    # → arm64
which brew                             # → /opt/homebrew/bin/brew
~/cohere_env/bin/python --version     # → Python 3.14.3
~/cohere_env/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())"  # → True
```

---

## Emergency Remediation

**If everything is broken, nuclear reset:**
```bash
# 1. Remove broken environment
rm -rf ~/cohere_env

# 2. Install native Homebrew (ask user for sudo)
arch -arm64 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# 3. Follow setup protocol from Phase 2
```

**Migration from x86_64 to arm64 Homebrew:**
1. Document existing packages: `brew list --formula > /tmp/x86_packages.txt`
2. Install native Homebrew
3. Reinstall packages with native brew
4. Remove old Homebrew: `sudo rm -rf /usr/local/Homebrew`

---

## Key Success Principles

1. **Always verify architecture** before proceeding
2. **Use `arch -arm64` prefix** for critical commands
3. **Validate at each phase** before continuing
4. **Fix symlinks** in virtual environments
5. **Don't modify original skill files** - use them as-is
6. **When in doubt, start fresh** with native tools

This protocol prevents the cascade failures that occur when wrong architecture components interact with each other.
